learning_outcomes.description: Communicate in both spoken and written Japanese on course-related topics, using appropriate patterns, vocabulary, script, and culturally appropriate expressions.
Use patterns, script, and vocabulary taught in the course to communicate on new topics and ideas.
Comprehend variation in Japanese language use and the diversity of its users across social, regional, and cultural contexts.
Reflect on and respond to feedback on your written and spoken Japanese and use this to improve your proficiency.

assessments.task_description:
All students who have not completed JAPN3030 at UQ are required to demonstrate that they have been correctly placed in JAPN3040. Students who have completed the prerequisite course JAPN3030 are not required to retake the questionnaire.

Use of AI / MT in this assessment

While this is an unsupervised, individual online task, it is not in your best interest to use generative AI tools or Machine Translation (MT). If the work you submit does not reflect your actual language ability, you may be placed in a course that is not appropriate for your level, which could negatively affect your learning and performance. This may also be considered an act of student misconduct under the UQ Student Code of Conduct due to the misrepresentation of your capabilities.

This requirement aims to ensure that you are placed in the most appropriate language course based on your current level of linguistic proficiency. Please note that all language courses include at least one assessment task where the use of generative AI or MT tools is either not allowed or of limited value. These assessments are designed to be authentic, challenging, and based on real-time interaction. You will be expected to demonstrate a clear understanding of your own work without the assistance of AI or MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Worksheets are designed to support continuous and active learning throughout the semester. They contain a variety of tasks that are integrated into class activities. Students will work on some parts of the worksheets during TUT2, where questions may be discussed, model answers reviewed, and feedback provided. Participation in these activities forms an important part of the learning process.

There will be 10 worksheets worth 3% each, plus one ungraded practice worksheet. Only the best 7 out of 10 worksheets will count towards the final grade, allowing students to miss up to three submissions without penalty.

All worksheet submissions must be original work and must not contain material submitted for assessment in other courses.

Worksheets will be distributed in hard copy during Tutorial 1. Students are expected to begin working on them in class and then complete, review, and revise them independently before submitting them by the specified due date.

Copies of all worksheets will also be available in the Assessment folder on Blackboard for students who are unable to attend class.

Use of AI / MT in this assessment

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students must handwrite the answers on worksheets in Japanese using pens, pencils, and erasers. Upon completion, they should scan the required pages in the portrait direction (e.g., using the Scanner app or scanner) and upload the scanned document in PDF format to the appropriate link in the “Worksheets” folder in the Assessment section of Blackboard. Typed worksheets do not meet the assessment requirements.

assessments.task_description:
Students will complete an in-person oral assessment consisting of a three-minute conversational dialogue in Japanese based on one or two assigned topics.

At the beginning of the assessment, students will be provided with a card outlining the conversational setting and key information to incorporate into the dialogue. Students will have two minutes to review the prompt before commencing. An examiner will participate in the dialogue by taking the role of a conversation partner.

The dialogue should demonstrate students' understanding and use of the language covered in Chapters 6 and 7. Students are expected to incorporate relevant vocabulary and grammatical structures from these chapters, as well as language from earlier chapters where appropriate. Students should also demonstrate their ability to use the language creatively and appropriately within the given context.

Following the dialogue, the examiner will ask questions in Japanese related to the conversation for approximately two minutes. The assessment will take approximately seven minutes per student in total.

Important Notes

The presentation should not be read from a script. It should be presented at a natural speed, spoken in a way that conveys meaning. This is a closed-book assessment item. You are not allowed to use reference materials. Your work must be authentic; that is, receiving help from others is prohibited.

Marking

Students' oral performance will be assessed based on content, delivery, and language, such as pronunciation, vocabulary, and grammar. Please see the marking rubric on Blackboard. Students with Alternative Exam Adjustments in place should contact the Course Coordinator directly and apply through Unitask to activate them for this assessment.

Use of AI / MT in this assessment

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
In Oral Presentation 2, students will complete an in-person oral assessment consisting of a three-minute conversational dialogue in Japanese based on a given topic.

At the beginning of the assessment, students will be provided with a card outlining the conversational setting and key information to incorporate into the dialogue. Students will have two minutes to review the prompt before commencing. An examiner will participate in the dialogue by taking the role of a conversation partner.

The dialogue should demonstrate students' understanding and use of the language covered in Chapters 8 and 9. Students are expected to incorporate relevant vocabulary and grammatical structures from these chapters, as well as language from earlier chapters where appropriate. Students should also demonstrate their ability to use the language creatively and appropriately within the given context.

Following the dialogue, the examiner will ask questions in Japanese related to the conversation for approximately one to two minutes. The assessment will take approximately seven minutes per student in total.

Important Notes

The presentation should not be read from a script—it should be presented at a natural speed, spoken in a way that conveys meaning. This is a closed-book assessment item. You are not allowed to use reference materials. Your work must be authentic; that is, receiving help from others is prohibited.

Marking

Students' oral performance will be assessed based on content, delivery, and language, such as pronunciation, vocabulary, and grammar. Please see the marking rubric on Blackboard.

Students with Alternative Exam Adjustments in place should contact the Course Coordinator directly and apply through Unitask to activate them for this assessment.

Use of AI / MT in this assessment

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment is designed to consolidate and evaluate students' learning throughout the course. The final exam is centrally organised.

The examination consists of the following components:

Grammar: Application of course content, assessing students' understanding and use of the key language structures covered in Chapters 6–10, including material from the textbook, class slides, worksheets, and Tutorial 1 sentence-writing activities

Writing: Students to produce a short piece of writing on a given topic. The writing task will be assessed on organisation, grammatical accuracy, vocabulary range, orthography, appropriate forms, and writing style. The assessment rubric will be available on Blackboard.

Students with Alternative Exam Adjustments should contact the Course Coordinator and submit the relevant request through UniTask to activate their adjustments for this assessment.

Use of AI / MT in this assessment

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorials

In-class tutorial work: Every class hour introduces new materials and builds on the material from the previous ones. Regular preparation, attendance, and active participation are key to successful learning and progress in the course. Readings/Ref: Textbook, Bb site.
